profile.name: Thảo Nguyên Trần
profile.title: Data Analyst · Risk Management · Quantitative Research
profile.tagline: Mathematical Economics graduate bridging quantitative analysis and high-stakes financial decision-making. Specialized in credit risk modeling, fraud detection, and statistical early-warning systems. Turning complex data into defensible risk judgments for banking and financial institutions.
profile.bio: Trained in Mathematical Economics with hands-on research across credit risk, banking performance, and market risk modeling. I use statistical and machine learning methods including panel data regression, Bayesian inference, and classification models to quantify uncertainty and support high-stakes decisions in finance, risk management, and economic policy. My work spans published research, regulatory-aligned frameworks (Basel II/III, ISO 31000), and production-ready risk pipelines.

about.background: Bachelor of Mathematical Economics — University of Economics and Law, VNU-HCM (Nov 2021 – July 2025) · Exchange Student - HSB Hochschule Bremen, Germany (Oct 2023 - Feb 2024)
about.professional_focus: Risk Analysis, Data Analytics, and Business Intelligence - with emphasis on statistical modeling, machine learning, and quantitative research.

experience.title: Quantitative Research Specialist
experience.company: Hung Vuong University, Ho Chi Minh City
highlights: Collected, stored, and queried market data using SQL for internal analytics platform with 100% data integrity
Built early-warning system for VN-Index and sectoral indices — generated sell signals 2–4 weeks before major corrections
Reduced maximum drawdown from 47% to 18% in backtests through improved portfolio risk management
Standardized financial and regulatory data into auditable, reproducible formats for AI research project

experience.title: Risk Management & Planning Intern
experience.company: Vietnam Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (Agribank)
highlights: Built fraud detection models (Random Forest, SMOTE) on 284,807 credit card transactions
Improved fraud recall while reducing false positives to only 10 flagged legitimate transactions
Analyzed transaction patterns to identify anomalies and provide early-warning insights to risk monitoring team
Compiled weekly portfolio data, flagged documentation inconsistencies across Credit, Client, and Audit departments

projects.title: Credit Risk Analysis at Agribank: Classification Techniques for Fraud Detection
projects.context: Modernizing credit risk early-warning systems at Vietnam's largest agricultural bank using ML classification for credit card fraud detection.
methodology: Compared Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest
results: Random Forest: 99.95% Accuracy, 84.16% Recall, only 10 false positives
Identified payment history & collateral assets as top 2 risk factors
Proposed AI/ML-integrated early warning system — well-received by bank leadership

projects.title: Walmart India E-commerce Platform: Data Visualization & Storytelling
projects.context: Analyzed customer behavior on Walmart India's platform to extract business insights using data visualization and storytelling techniques.
methodology: Multi-dimensional analysis: demographic, product, payment, location
results: Electronics: 45M INR revenue (highest), Clothing 30M INR, Beauty & Health 25M INR
Age group 25–45 contributed >60M INR — 3x the 45–60 group
Credit Card: >40% of payments; UPI/digital wallet growing at 14.6%
Mumbai & Delhi lead revenue; Dehradun & Srinagar identified as untapped growth markets
December peak: ~18M INR — strong seasonal promotion opportunity

projects.title: Internal Banking IT Applications & Credit Risk: Ridge Regression Analysis
projects.context: Investigated how Core Banking, basic IT applications, and e-payment systems affect credit risk in Vietnamese commercial banks.
methodology: 3 independent variables: Core Banking, Basic IT, E-Payment Index
Feature Importance via Mean Dropout Loss
results: Validation MSE = 0.423, Test MSE = 0.363, MAE = 0.457
Core Banking & IT applications → reduced credit risk (negative coefficients)
E-payment → increased credit risk (β = +0.206) — critical policy finding
Basic IT Application had highest feature importance (0.837)

projects.title: AI Impact on the Sharing Economy: GSCA Structural Analysis
projects.context: Investigated how AI transforms sharing economy models (transportation, accommodation, services) and which sectors benefit most.
methodology: EFA with ULS & Promax rotation + GSCA modeling
results: Shared transportation AI: β = 0.385 (strongest impact)
All paths statistically significant at 95% confidence
Schedule optimization (0.727) & device sharing (0.748) showed highest AI value

projects.title: Credit Risk in Vietnam's Commercial Banks: Panel Data Regression
projects.context: Analyzed factors influencing credit risk across 27 Vietnamese commercial banks. Credit risk represents ~54% of total banking system risk.
methodology: 11 variables: past credit risk, bank size, capital, GDP, inflation, unemployment
OLS, FEM, REM comparison + GLS correction
results: Lagged credit risk β = 0.496 (strongest positive predictor)
Inflation β = 0.0016, Unemployment β = 0.0025 (both positive)
Bank size β = −0.0014 (larger banks → lower risk)
Policy solutions proposed based on Basel II & III frameworks

projects.title: Stock Market Bubble Detection: Multifractal Analysis + Behavioral Signals
projects.context: VN-Index crashed 35% in 6 months in 2018 and 2022. Can we detect bubbles earlier by combining market complexity with behavioral signals?
methodology: MFDFA → 3 features: Δα, Hurst Exponent (H), Asymmetry (A)
Random Forest + XGBoost, SVM, Neural Networks + SHAP explainability
results: Integrated model: F1 = 97.5%, AUC = 0.992
vs. multifractal-only (F1 = 84.7%) or behavioral-only (F1 = 72.3%)
Trading strategy Sharpe ratio: 1.87 vs. 0.94 (buy-and-hold)
Maximum drawdown reduced: 47% → 18%
Proposed 3-tier alert framework for State Securities Commission: Yellow / Orange / Red
